channel.title: Cortex-M on CodetoCore
channel.description: Recent content in Cortex-M on CodetoCore

item.title: Is SysTick Accurate Enough for Microsecond Timing?
item.description: Short answer: the counter is. Everything around it usually isn't.

item.title: What TrustZone-M Adds to Your Build
item.description: Why moving from a plain Cortex-M to a TrustZone-M part doubles your project structure — and what each new piece is actually doing.

item.title: What Actually Happens Before main() Runs?
item.description: A lot. The CPU walks through six setup stages between power-up and your first line of C — and bugs in any of them look very different from each other.
